moon 0.1.0
  • Reference

    • Customizing MOON
  • Changelog

MOON parameter spec class system

Source: R/moon-params.R
moon_param-methods.Rd

S3 generics dispatched on moon_param_* spec classes. moon_param_value() returns the deterministic point value of a spec — used internally as a safety net when a spec is encountered in a deterministic context. moon_param_sample() returns n random draws and accepts auxiliary shared-randomness vectors (z for lognormal HRs, u for gamma costs) so moon_sample_params() can reuse one set of random numbers across multiple specs to reproduce the published MOON "one-world" convention.

Usage

moon_param_value(x, ...)

moon_param_sample(x, n, ...)

Arguments

x

A spec object inheriting from moon_param.

...

Additional arguments. Lognormal methods accept z (a length-n rnorm() vector); gamma methods accept u (a length-n runif() vector); both default to NULL.

n

Integer; number of random draws to generate.

Value

moon_param_value() returns a numeric (vector or scalar depending on the spec). moon_param_sample() returns a length-n vector for scalar specs, or an n-row matrix for vector-valued specs.

Details

When the auxiliary argument is NULL (the default) each spec generates its own independent draws.

See also

the constructors: moon_param_fixed(), moon_param_lognormal(), moon_param_gamma(), moon_param_mvnorm(), moon_param_dirichlet().

On this page

Developed by Nicolai Damslund, Gudrun M W Bjørnelv, Yufei Jiang, Magnus Skov Larsen, Christina Hansen Edwards, Vidar Halsteinli, Rønnaug A Ødegaard, Line Planck Kongstad.
